introductionSomali: Unit-kan wuxuu barayaa sida wada-hadal loo bilaabo loona sii wado, codsiyo iyo oggolaansho edeb leh, mahadcelin, raalli-gelin, casuumaad, talo, heshiis iyo khilaaf, telefoon, fariimo iyo tone ku habboon.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Salaamaha xaaladaha kala duwan waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Greetings in Different Situations.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Good morning” while meeting someone at a course, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Good morning” si ay u muujiso “subax wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: greeting. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa subax wanaagsan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Good afternoon” while meeting someone at a course.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Good afternoon” si ay u muujiso “galab wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: greeting. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa galab wanaagsan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.

vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Good evening” si ay u muujiso “fiid wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: greeting. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fiid wanaagsan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “How are you?” while meeting someone at a course.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: greeting. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa sidee tahay? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.

vocabulary.englishWord: It is nice to meet you.
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: waan ku faraxsanahay inaan kula kulmo
vocabulary.pronunciation: it is nice to meet you.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “It is nice to meet you.” while meeting someone at a course.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: introduction. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ku faraxsanahay inaan kula kulmo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Greetings in Different Situations: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Salaamaha xaaladaha kala duwan
grammar.explanationSomali: Choose a greeting that matches the time, relationship, and level of formality. Good night usually ends an evening conversation. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Choose a greeting that matches the time, relationship, and level of formality. Good night usually ends an evening conversation.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: meeting someone at a course • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Good morning
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Good morning.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Good evening
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Good evening.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: How have you been?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: How have you been?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan meeting someone at a course. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan meeting someone at a course. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Salaamaha xaaladaha kala duwan: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka greeting + suitable response, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Isbarashada iyo isku-baridda dadka waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Introducing Yourself and Others.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “My name is Samira.” while introducing people at a fictional training session, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “My name is Samira.” si ay u muujiso “magacaygu waa...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: introduction. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa magacaygu waa... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I am Samira.” while introducing people at a fictional training session.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am Samira.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan ahay...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: introduction. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaan ahay... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Let me introduce myself.” while introducing people at a fictional training session because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Let me introduce myself.” si ay u muujiso “aan isbaro”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: introduction. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aan isbaro iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: kani waa qofka aan wada shaqayno...
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “This is my colleague, Amina.” while introducing people at a fictional training session.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “This is my colleague, Amina.” si ay u muujiso “kani waa qofka aan wada shaqayno...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: introduction. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa kani waa qofka aan wada shaqayno... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Have you met Hassan?” while introducing people at a fictional training session, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Have you met Hassan?” si ay u muujiso “hore ma ula kulantay...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: introduction. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa hore ma ula kulantay...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I would like you to meet...
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: waxaan jeclaan lahaa inaad la kulanto...

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: introduction. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaan jeclaan lahaa inaad la kulanto... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “We work together.” while introducing people at a fictional training session because it matched the situation.

vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I have heard a lot about you.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I have heard a lot about you.” while introducing people at a fictional training session, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I have heard a lot about you.” si ay u muujiso “wax badan ayaan kaa maqlay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: introduction. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa wax badan ayaan kaa maqlay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Recently joined” while introducing people at a fictional training session and chose a suitable response.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Recently joined”, oo macnihiisu yahay “dhowaan ku biiray”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: information. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa dhowaan ku biiray iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Training session” while introducing people at a fictional training session, so the meaning was clear.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: information. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadhiga tababarka iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Customer service” in her notes while introducing people at a fictional training session and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Customer service”, oo macnihiisu yahay “adeegga macaamiisha”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: information. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa adeegga macaamiisha iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Introducing Yourself and Others: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Isbarashada iyo isku-baridda dadka
grammar.explanationSomali: Use Present Simple for names, jobs, classes, and other general information. Use who only for a short A2 description of a person. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Use Present Simple for names, jobs, classes, and other general information. Use who only for a short A2 description of a person.
grammar.sentenceStructure: This is + person; I would like you to meet + person

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: introducing people at a fictional training session • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: My name is Samira.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: My name is Samira..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Let me introduce myself.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Let me introduce myself..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Have you met Hassan?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Have you met Hassan?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “I am Samira.”. Function: Introducing Yourself and Others. Tone-ka iyo context-ka ayaa jawaabtan ka dhigaya tan keliya ee ugu habboon. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg Introducing Yourself and Others.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan introducing people at a fictional training session. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan introducing people at a fictional training session. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Isbarashada iyo isku-baridda dadka: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka This is + person; I would like you to meet + person, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Bilaabista wada sheekeysiga fudud waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Starting Small Talk.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “How was your journey?” while starting safe small talk before an event, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “How was your journey?” si ay u muujiso “sidee safarkaagu ahaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: small talk. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa sidee safarkaagu ahaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Starting Small Talk: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Bilaabista wada sheekeysiga fudud
grammar.explanationSomali: Small talk uses safe shared topics. Avoid private questions about income, health, politics, religion, relationships, or age. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Small talk uses safe shared topics. Avoid private questions about income, health, politics, religion, relationships, or age.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: starting safe small talk before an event • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: How was your journey?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: How was your journey?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What do you think of the event?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: What do you think of the event?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: It is quite busy today, is not it?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: It is quite busy today, is not it?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan starting safe small talk before an event. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan starting safe small talk before an event. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Bilaabista wada sheekeysiga fudud: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka safe observation + open question, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Sii wadista wada sheekeysiga waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Keeping a Conversation Going.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Really?” while asking a useful follow-up question, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Really?” si ay u muujiso “runtii?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: active listening. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa runtii? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: taasi waxay u muuqataa mid xiiso leh
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “That sounds interesting.” while asking a useful follow-up question.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: active listening. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi waxay u muuqataa mid xiiso leh iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Keeping a Conversation Going: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Sii wadista wada sheekeysiga
grammar.explanationSomali: Follow-up questions connect directly to what the other person said. Listening responses must match good or difficult news. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Follow-up questions connect directly to what the other person said. Listening responses must match good or difficult news.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Really?” while asking a useful follow-up question, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Really?” si ay u muujiso “runtii?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What happened next?” si ay u muujiso “maxaa xigay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “How did you feel?” while asking a useful follow-up question.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “How did you feel?” si ay u muujiso “sideed dareentay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “What did you do after that?” while asking a useful follow-up question, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What did you do after that?” si ay u muujiso “maxaad samaysay intaas ka dib?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “How long have you done that?” while asking a useful follow-up question.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “How long have you done that?” si ay u muujiso “intee ayaad taas samaynaysay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “What do you like about it?” while asking a useful follow-up question because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What do you like about it?” si ay u muujiso “maxaad ka jeceshahay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Why did you choose it?” while asking a useful follow-up question.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Why did you choose it?” si ay u muujiso “maxaad u dooratay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: asking a useful follow-up question • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Really?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Really?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What happened next?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: What happened next?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What did you do after that?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: What did you do after that?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan asking a useful follow-up question. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan asking a useful follow-up question. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Sii wadista wada sheekeysiga: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka listening response + relevant follow-up question, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Su’aalaha furan iyo kuwa xiran waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Asking Open and Closed Questions.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Closed question” while choosing a question that invites the right amount of information, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Closed question”, oo macnihiisu yahay “su’aal xiran”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: question type. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa su’aal xiran iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Open question” while choosing a question that invites the right amount of information and chose a suitable response.

grammar.titleEnglish: Asking Open and Closed Questions: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Su’aalaha furan iyo kuwa xiran
grammar.explanationSomali: Closed questions can be answered briefly. Open questions usually begin with what, how, why, where, or when and invite more detail. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Closed questions can be answered briefly. Open questions usually begin with what, how, why, where, or when and invite more detail.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: choosing a question that invites the right amount of information • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Closed question
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Closed question.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Do you work here?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Do you work here?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Are you free tomorrow?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Are you free tomorrow?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan choosing a question that invites the right amount of information. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan choosing a question that invites the right amount of information. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Su’aalaha furan iyo kuwa xiran: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka auxiliary + subject...?; WH-word + auxiliary + subject...?, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Muujinta xiisaha iyo dhegeysiga wanaagsan waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Showing Interest and Active Listening.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I see.” while listening without interrupting a classmate, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I see.” si ay u muujiso “waan fahmay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: active listening. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan fahmay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I understand.” while listening without interrupting a classmate.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I understand.” si ay u muujiso “waan fahmay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: active listening. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan fahmay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “That is interesting.” while listening without interrupting a classmate because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is interesting.” si ay u muujiso “taasi waa xiiso”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: active listening. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi waa xiiso iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Go on.” while listening without interrupting a classmate.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Go on.” si ay u muujiso “sii wad”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: active listening. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa sii wad iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Tell me more.” while listening without interrupting a classmate, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Tell me more.” si ay u muujiso “wax dheeraad ah ii sheeg”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: active listening. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa wax dheeraad ah ii sheeg iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “That must have been difficult.” while listening without interrupting a classmate.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That must have been difficult.” si ay u muujiso “taasi waa inay adkayd”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: empathy. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi waa inay adkayd iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Smile” while listening without interrupting a classmate, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Smile”, oo macnihiisu yahay “dhoolla-caddee”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: body language. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa dhoolla-caddee iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Nod” while listening without interrupting a classmate and chose a suitable response.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Nod”, oo macnihiisu yahay “madaxa u rux oggolaansho”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: body language. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa madaxa u rux oggolaansho iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Wait for your turn” while listening without interrupting a classmate, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Wait for your turn”, oo macnihiisu yahay “sug markaga”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: listening. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa sug markaga iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Avoid interrupting” in her notes while listening without interrupting a classmate and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Avoid interrupting”, oo macnihiisu yahay “iska ilaali hadal dhex-gelinta”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: listening. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa iska ilaali hadal dhex-gelinta iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Showing Interest and Active Listening: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Muujinta xiisaha iyo dhegeysiga wanaagsan
grammar.explanationSomali: Active listening combines short verbal responses with patient attention. Non-verbal customs differ across cultures. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Active listening combines short verbal responses with patient attention. Non-verbal customs differ across cultures.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I see.” while listening without interrupting a classmate, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I see.” si ay u muujiso “waan fahmay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: listening without interrupting a classmate • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I see.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I see..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: That is interesting.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: That is interesting..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Tell me more.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Tell me more..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan listening without interrupting a classmate. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan listening without interrupting a classmate. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Muujinta xiisaha iyo dhegeysiga wanaagsan: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka appropriate response + relevant question, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.titleEnglish: Polite Requests With Can and Could
lessons.titleSomali: Codsiyada edebta leh ee Can iyo Could
lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Codsiyada edebta leh ee Can iyo Could waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Polite Requests With Can and Could.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Can you help me?” while asking a classmate for practical help, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can you help me?” si ay u muujiso “ma i caawin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma i caawin kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Could you help me with this form?
vocabulary.pronunciation: could you help me with this form?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Could you help me with this form?” while asking a classmate for practical help.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could you help me with this form?” si ay u muujiso “foomkan ma iga caawin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa foomkan ma iga caawin kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Can you open the window?” while asking a classmate for practical help because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can you open the window?” si ay u muujiso “daaqadda ma furi kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa daaqadda ma furi kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: si tartiib ah ma u hadli kartaa?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Could you speak more slowly?” while asking a classmate for practical help.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could you speak more slowly?” si ay u muujiso “si tartiib ah ma u hadli kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa si tartiib ah ma u hadli kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Can you send me the address?
vocabulary.pronunciation: can you send me the address?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Can you send me the address?” while asking a classmate for practical help, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can you send me the address?” si ay u muujiso “cinwaanka ma ii diri kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa cinwaanka ma ii diri kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: fadlan taas ma ku celin kartaa?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Could you repeat that, please?” while asking a classmate for practical help.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could you repeat that, please?” si ay u muujiso “fadlan taas ma ku celin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan taas ma ku celin kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Yes, of course.” while asking a classmate for practical help because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Yes, of course.” si ay u muujiso “haa, dabcan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept request. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa haa, dabcan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Certainly.” while asking a classmate for practical help.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Certainly.” si ay u muujiso “hubaal”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept request. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa hubaal iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “No problem.” while asking a classmate for practical help, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “No problem.” si ay u muujiso “dhib ma leh”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept request. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa dhib ma leh iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I cannot right now.” while asking a classmate for practical help.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I cannot right now.” si ay u muujiso “hadda ma awoodo”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: decline request. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa hadda ma awoodo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “In a few minutes” while asking a classmate for practical help because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “In a few minutes” si ay u muujiso “dhowr daqiiqo gudahood”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: time response. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa dhowr daqiiqo gudahood iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Please” while asking a classmate for practical help.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please” si ay u muujiso “fadlan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: politeness marker. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Polite Requests With Can and Could: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Codsiyada edebta leh ee Can iyo Could
grammar.explanationSomali: Can makes an ordinary request. Could often sounds softer or more polite. Both are followed by the base verb. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Can makes an ordinary request. Could often sounds softer or more polite. Both are followed by the base verb.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Can you help me?” while asking a classmate for practical help, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can you help me?” si ay u muujiso “ma i caawin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can you send me the address?” si ay u muujiso “cinwaanka ma ii diri kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Could you repeat that, please?” while asking a classmate for practical help.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could you repeat that, please?” si ay u muujiso “fadlan taas ma ku celin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Yes, of course.” while asking a classmate for practical help because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Yes, of course.” si ay u muujiso “haa, dabcan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Certainly.” while asking a classmate for practical help.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Certainly.” si ay u muujiso “hubaal”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: asking a classmate for practical help • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Can you help me?

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan asking a classmate for practical help. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan asking a classmate for practical help. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Codsiyada edebta leh ee Can iyo Could: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka Can/Could + subject + base verb + please?, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.titleEnglish: Would You Mind and Do You Mind
lessons.titleSomali: Codsiyada Would You Mind iyo Do You Mind
lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Codsiyada Would You Mind iyo Do You Mind waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Would You Mind and Do You Mind.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.englishWord: Would you mind closing the door?
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad albaabka xirto?
vocabulary.pronunciation: would you mind closing the door?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Would you mind closing the door while the class is recording?
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad albaabka xirto inta fasalku wax duubayo?
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad albaabka xirto? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Do you mind waiting for a moment?
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad xoogaa sugto?
vocabulary.pronunciation: do you mind waiting for a moment?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Do you mind waiting for a moment?” while making a careful request in a shared room.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you mind waiting for a moment?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad xoogaa sugto?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad xoogaa sugto? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Would you mind speaking more quietly?
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad cod hoose ku hadasho?
vocabulary.pronunciation: would you mind speaking more quietly?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Would you mind speaking more quietly?” while making a careful request in a shared room because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you mind speaking more quietly?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad cod hoose ku hadasho?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad cod hoose ku hadasho? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Do you mind if I sit here?
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaan halkan fariisto?
vocabulary.pronunciation: do you mind if i sit here?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Do you mind if I sit here?” while making a careful request in a shared room.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you mind if I sit here?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaan halkan fariisto?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaan halkan fariisto? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Would you mind if I used your phone?
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa haddii aan telefoonkaaga isticmaalo?
vocabulary.pronunciation: would you mind if i used your phone?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Would you mind if I used your phone?” while making a careful request in a shared room, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you mind if I used your phone?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa haddii aan telefoonkaaga isticmaalo?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa haddii aan telefoonkaaga isticmaalo? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “No, not at all.” while making a careful request in a shared room.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “No, not at all.” si ay u muujiso “maya, dhib ma leh”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept request. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa maya, dhib ma leh iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Of course not.” while making a careful request in a shared room because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Of course not.” si ay u muujiso “dabcan maya; dhib ma leh”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept request. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa dabcan maya; dhib ma leh iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “That is fine.” while making a careful request in a shared room.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is fine.” si ay u muujiso “taasi waa hagaag”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept request. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi waa hagaag iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Go ahead.” while making a careful request in a shared room, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Go ahead.” si ay u muujiso “samee; waad samayn kartaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa samee; waad samayn kartaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I need it right now.” while making a careful request in a shared room.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I need it right now.” si ay u muujiso “hadda ayaan u baahanahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: decline request. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa hadda ayaan u baahanahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I am afraid that is not possible.
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: waxaan ka xumahay inaysan suurtagal ahayn
vocabulary.pronunciation: i am afraid that is not possible.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “I am afraid that is not possible.” while making a careful request in a shared room because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am afraid that is not possible.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan ka xumahay inaysan suurtagal ahayn”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: decline request. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaan ka xumahay inaysan suurtagal ahayn iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Verb-ing” in her notes while making a careful request in a shared room and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Verb-ing”, oo macnihiisu yahay “fal ku dhammaada ing”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: grammar. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fal ku dhammaada ing iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Would You Mind and Do You Mind: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Codsiyada Would You Mind iyo Do You Mind
grammar.explanationSomali: Would you mind is followed by verb-ing. Do you mind if is followed by a subject and Present Simple. No, not at all means the request is accepted. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Would you mind is followed by verb-ing. Do you mind if is followed by a subject and Present Simple. No, not at all means the request is accepted.
grammar.sentenceStructure: Would you mind + verb-ing?; Do you mind if + subject + present verb?

positiveExamples.english: Would you mind closing the door while the class is recording?
positiveExamples.somali: Ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad albaabka xirto inta fasalku wax duubayo?

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Do you mind waiting for a moment?” while making a careful request in a shared room.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you mind waiting for a moment?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad xoogaa sugto?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Would you mind speaking more quietly?” while making a careful request in a shared room because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you mind speaking more quietly?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad cod hoose ku hadasho?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Do you mind if I sit here?” while making a careful request in a shared room.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you mind if I sit here?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaan halkan fariisto?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Would you mind if I used your phone?” while making a careful request in a shared room, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you mind if I used your phone?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa haddii aan telefoonkaaga isticmaalo?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “No, not at all.” while making a careful request in a shared room.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “No, not at all.” si ay u muujiso “maya, dhib ma leh”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Of course not.” while making a careful request in a shared room because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Of course not.” si ay u muujiso “dabcan maya; dhib ma leh”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “That is fine.” while making a careful request in a shared room.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is fine.” si ay u muujiso “taasi waa hagaag”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

practiceQuestions.question: Choose the accurate form for this Would You Mind and Do You Mind situation (1).
options: Would you mind speaking more quietly?
A response with the wrong tone.
practiceQuestions.correctAnswer: Would you mind speaking more quietly?
practiceQuestions.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Would you mind speaking more quietly?”. Function: Would You Mind and Do You Mind. Tone-ka iyo context-ka ayaa jawaabtan ka dhigaya tan keliya ee ugu habboon. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg Would You Mind and Do You Mind.

practiceQuestions.question: Choose the accurate form for this Would You Mind and Do You Mind situation (2).
options: Do you mind if I sit here?

examples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Do you mind waiting for a moment?” while making a careful request in a shared room.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you mind waiting for a moment?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad xoogaa sugto?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: The trainer chose “Would you mind speaking more quietly?” while making a careful request in a shared room because it matched the situation.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you mind speaking more quietly?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib ayay kugu noqonaysaa inaad cod hoose ku hadasho?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Do you mind if I sit here?” while making a careful request in a shared room.

examples.english: Amina said, “Would you mind if I used your phone?” while making a careful request in a shared room, and her partner responded appropriately.

examples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “No, not at all.” while making a careful request in a shared room.

examples.english: The trainer chose “Of course not.” while making a careful request in a shared room because it matched the situation.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Of course not.” si ay u muujiso “dabcan maya; dhib ma leh”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “That is fine.” while making a careful request in a shared room.

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Would you mind closing the door?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Would you mind closing the door?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Would you mind speaking more quietly?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Would you mind speaking more quietly?.

lines.speaker: Hassan

lines.speaker: Hassan

lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan making a careful request in a shared room. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan making a careful request in a shared room. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Codsiyada Would You Mind iyo Do You Mind: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka Would you mind + verb-ing?; Do you mind if + subject + present verb?, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Oggolaansho weydiisashada iyo bixinta waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Asking for and Giving Permission.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Can I sit here?” while asking permission at a fictional venue, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can I sit here?” si ay u muujiso “ma samayn karaa...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma samayn karaa...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Could I borrow this chair?” while asking permission at a fictional venue.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could I borrow this chair?” si ay u muujiso “ma samayn karaa...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma samayn karaa...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “May I enter the room?” while asking permission at a fictional venue because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I enter the room?” si ay u muujiso “ma ii bannaan tahay inaan...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma ii bannaan tahay inaan...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Is it okay if I leave early?” while asking permission at a fictional venue.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Is it okay if I leave early?” si ay u muujiso “ma hagaag baa haddii aan...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma hagaag baa haddii aan...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Do you mind if I open the window?” while asking permission at a fictional venue, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you mind if I open the window?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib baa haddii aan...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma dhib baa haddii aan...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ma la ii oggol yahay inaan...?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Am I allowed to take photographs here?” while asking permission at a fictional venue.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Am I allowed to take photographs here?” si ay u muujiso “ma la ii oggol yahay inaan...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma la ii oggol yahay inaan...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “May I come in?” while asking permission at a fictional venue because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I come in?” si ay u muujiso “ma soo geli karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma soo geli karaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Go ahead.” while asking permission at a fictional venue.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Go ahead.” si ay u muujiso “waad samayn kartaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: grant permission. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waad samayn kartaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “That is fine.” while asking permission at a fictional venue, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is fine.” si ay u muujiso “waa hagaag”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: grant permission. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waa hagaag iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “That is not allowed.” while asking permission at a fictional venue.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is not allowed.” si ay u muujiso “taasi lama oggola”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: refuse permission. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi lama oggola iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Manager permission” while asking permission at a fictional venue, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Manager permission”, oo macnihiisu yahay “oggolaanshaha maamulaha”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: permission. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa oggolaanshaha maamulaha iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Take photographs” in her notes while asking permission at a fictional venue and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Take photographs”, oo macnihiisu yahay “sawirro qaad”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: activity. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa sawirro qaad iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Asking for and Giving Permission: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Oggolaansho weydiisashada iyo bixinta
grammar.explanationSomali: Can and could ask ordinary permission; may is often more formal. A clear refusal can explain which rule or person controls permission. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Can and could ask ordinary permission; may is often more formal. A clear refusal can explain which rule or person controls permission.
grammar.sentenceStructure: Can/Could/May I + base verb?; Is it okay if + clause?
