positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you mind if I open the window?” si ay u muujiso “ma dhib baa haddii aan...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Am I allowed to take photographs here?” while asking permission at a fictional venue.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Am I allowed to take photographs here?” si ay u muujiso “ma la ii oggol yahay inaan...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “May I come in?” while asking permission at a fictional venue because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I come in?” si ay u muujiso “ma soo geli karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Go ahead.” while asking permission at a fictional venue.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Go ahead.” si ay u muujiso “waad samayn kartaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: asking permission at a fictional venue • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Can I sit here?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Can I sit here?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: May I enter the room?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: May I enter the room?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Do you mind if I open the window?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Do you mind if I open the window?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan asking permission at a fictional venue. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan asking permission at a fictional venue. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Oggolaansho weydiisashada iyo bixinta: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka Can/Could/May I + base verb?; Is it okay if + clause?, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Bixinta caawimada waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Offering Help.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Can I help you?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can I help you?” si ay u muujiso “ma ku caawin karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: offer. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma ku caawin karaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Shall I carry that for you?
vocabulary.pronunciation: shall i carry that for you?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Shall I carry that for you?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Shall I carry that for you?” si ay u muujiso “ma kuu qaadaa taas?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: offer. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma kuu qaadaa taas? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Would you like some help?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you like some help?” si ay u muujiso “caawimo ma jeclaan lahayd?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: offer. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa caawimo ma jeclaan lahayd? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Do you need a hand?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you need a hand?” si ay u muujiso “caawimo ma u baahan tahay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: offer. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa caawimo ma u baahan tahay? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I can show you where it is.
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: waxaan ku tusi karaa meesha ay tahay
vocabulary.pronunciation: i can show you where it is.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I can show you where it is.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I can show you where it is.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan ku tusi karaa meesha ay tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: offer. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaan ku tusi karaa meesha ay tahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Let me help you.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Let me help you.” si ay u muujiso “aan ku caawiyo”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: offer. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aan ku caawiyo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I will check that for you.
vocabulary.pronunciation: i will check that for you.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “I will check that for you.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I will check that for you.” si ay u muujiso “waan kuu hubinayaa taas”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: offer. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan kuu hubinayaa taas iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Would you like me to call someone?
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ma jeclaan lahayd inaan qof kuu waco?
vocabulary.pronunciation: would you like me to call someone?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Would you like me to call someone?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you like me to call someone?” si ay u muujiso “ma jeclaan lahayd inaan qof kuu waco?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: offer. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma jeclaan lahayd inaan qof kuu waco? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “That would be helpful.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That would be helpful.” si ay u muujiso “taasi way i caawin lahayd”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept offer. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi way i caawin lahayd iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I would appreciate that.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I would appreciate that.” si ay u muujiso “waan uga mahadcelin lahaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept offer. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan uga mahadcelin lahaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “I can manage.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I can manage.” si ay u muujiso “waan isku filanahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: decline offer. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan isku filanahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “That is kind of you.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is kind of you.” si ay u muujiso “taasi waa naxariis”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: response. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi waa naxariis iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Offering Help: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Bixinta caawimada
grammar.explanationSomali: Offers use can, shall, would like, or let me. The listener may accept or decline without sounding unfriendly. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Offers use can, shall, would like, or let me. The listener may accept or decline without sounding unfriendly.
grammar.sentenceStructure: Can/Shall I + base verb?; Would you like + noun/help?

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Can I help you?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can I help you?” si ay u muujiso “ma ku caawin karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Shall I carry that for you?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Shall I carry that for you?” si ay u muujiso “ma kuu qaadaa taas?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Would you like some help?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you like some help?” si ay u muujiso “caawimo ma jeclaan lahayd?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Do you need a hand?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you need a hand?” si ay u muujiso “caawimo ma u baahan tahay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I can show you where it is.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I can show you where it is.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan ku tusi karaa meesha ay tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Let me help you.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Let me help you.” si ay u muujiso “aan ku caawiyo”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “I will check that for you.” while offering help to someone carrying course materials because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I will check that for you.” si ay u muujiso “waan kuu hubinayaa taas”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Would you like me to call someone?” while offering help to someone carrying course materials.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you like me to call someone?” si ay u muujiso “ma jeclaan lahayd inaan qof kuu waco?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

options: A response with the wrong tone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: offering help to someone carrying course materials • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Can I help you?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Can I help you?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Would you like some help?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Would you like some help?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I can show you where it is.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I can show you where it is..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan offering help to someone carrying course materials. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan offering help to someone carrying course materials. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Bixinta caawimada: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka Can/Shall I + base verb?; Would you like + noun/help?, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Mahadcelinta waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Thanking People.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Thanks.” while thanking someone for useful course support, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thanks.” si ay u muujiso “mahadsanid”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa mahadsanid iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Thank you.” while thanking someone for useful course support.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thank you.” si ay u muujiso “waad mahadsan tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waad mahadsan tahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Thank you very much.” while thanking someone for useful course support because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thank you very much.” si ay u muujiso “aad baad u mahadsan tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aad baad u mahadsan tahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Thanks for your help.” while thanking someone for useful course support.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thanks for your help.” si ay u muujiso “waad ku mahadsan tahay caawimadaada”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waad ku mahadsan tahay caawimadaada iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: I really appreciate your help with the application form.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Runtii waan kaaga mahadcelinayaa caawimada aad iga siisay foomka codsiga.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa runtii waan uga mahadcelinayaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I appreciate your time.” while thanking someone for useful course support.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I appreciate your time.” si ay u muujiso “waan uga mahadcelinayaa waqtigaaga”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan uga mahadcelinayaa waqtigaaga iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “That was very kind of you.” while thanking someone for useful course support because it matched the situation.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi naxariis ayay kaa ahayd iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I am grateful for your support.
vocabulary.pronunciation: i am grateful for your support.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan uga mahadcelinayaa taageeradaada iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “You are welcome.” while thanking someone for useful course support, and her partner responded appropriately.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks response. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa adaa mudan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “My pleasure.” while thanking someone for useful course support.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “My pleasure.” si ay u muujiso “farxaddayda ayay ahayd”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks response. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa farxaddayda ayay ahayd iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ha ka hadlin; dhib ma leh
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Do not mention it.” while thanking someone for useful course support because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do not mention it.” si ay u muujiso “ha ka hadlin; dhib ma leh”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks response. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ha ka hadlin; dhib ma leh iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I am glad I could help.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks response. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ku faraxsanahay inaan ku caawiyay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Thanking People: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Mahadcelinta
grammar.explanationSomali: Thank someone for a noun or verb-ing. Appreciate is commonly followed by a noun, pronoun, or action, not directly by a person. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Thank someone for a noun or verb-ing. Appreciate is commonly followed by a noun, pronoun, or action, not directly by a person.
grammar.sentenceStructure: Thank + person + for + noun/verb-ing; appreciate + noun/action

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Thanks.” while thanking someone for useful course support, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thanks.” si ay u muujiso “mahadsanid”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Thank you.” while thanking someone for useful course support.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thank you.” si ay u muujiso “waad mahadsan tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Thank you very much.” while thanking someone for useful course support because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thank you very much.” si ay u muujiso “aad baad u mahadsan tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Thanks for your help.” while thanking someone for useful course support.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thanks for your help.” si ay u muujiso “waad ku mahadsan tahay caawimadaada”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: I really appreciate your help with the application form.
positiveExamples.somali: Runtii waan kaaga mahadcelinayaa caawimada aad iga siisay foomka codsiga.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I appreciate your time.” while thanking someone for useful course support.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I appreciate your time.” si ay u muujiso “waan uga mahadcelinayaa waqtigaaga”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “That was very kind of you.” while thanking someone for useful course support because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That was very kind of you.” si ay u muujiso “taasi naxariis ayay kaa ahayd”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I am grateful for your support.” while thanking someone for useful course support.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am grateful for your support.” si ay u muujiso “waan uga mahadcelinayaa taageeradaada”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

practiceQuestions.question: Choose the accurate form for this Thanking People situation (1).
options: A response with the wrong tone.
practiceQuestions.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Thank you very much.”. Function: Thanking People. Tone-ka iyo context-ka ayaa jawaabtan ka dhigaya tan keliya ee ugu habboon. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg Thanking People.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: thanking someone for useful course support • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Thanks.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Thanks..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Thank you very much.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Thank you very much..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I really appreciate it.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I really appreciate it..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan thanking someone for useful course support. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan thanking someone for useful course support. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Mahadcelinta: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka Thank + person + for + noun/verb-ing; appreciate + noun/action, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Raalli-gelinta waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Apologizing.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Sorry.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Sorry.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka xumahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ka xumahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I am very sorry.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am very sorry.” si ay u muujiso “aad ayaan uga xumahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aad ayaan uga xumahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “I apologize.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I apologize.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka raalli-gelinayaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ka raalli-gelinayaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ka xumahay daahitaanka iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am sorry that I forgot.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka xumahay inaan illoobay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ka xumahay inaan illoobay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.pronunciation: i am sorry about the mistake.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ka xumahay khaladka iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Please accept my apology.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake because it matched the situation.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan aqbal raalli-gelintayda iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I did not mean to interrupt you.
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: uma aan jeedin inaan hadalka kaa dhex galo
vocabulary.pronunciation: i did not mean to interrupt you.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I did not mean to interrupt you.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I did not mean to interrupt you.” si ay u muujiso “uma aan jeedin inaan hadalka kaa dhex galo”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa uma aan jeedin inaan hadalka kaa dhex galo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: I apologized to the customer for sending the wrong document.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Waxaan macmiilka uga raalli-geliyay inaan u diray dukumentigii khaldan.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology pattern. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa qof raalli-gelin ka sii iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Apologize for an action” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Apologize for an action”, oo macnihiisu yahay “ficil raalli-gelin ka bixi”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology pattern. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ficil raalli-gelin ka bixi iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Wrong document” in her notes while apologizing for a small everyday mistake and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Wrong document”, oo macnihiisu yahay “dukumenti khaldan”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: mistake. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa dukumenti khaldan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Apologizing: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Raalli-gelinta
grammar.explanationSomali: Use sorry for with a noun or verb-ing, sorry about with a noun, and apologize to a person for an action. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Use sorry for with a noun or verb-ing, sorry about with a noun, and apologize to a person for an action.
grammar.sentenceStructure: sorry for + noun/verb-ing; apologize to + person + for + action

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Sorry.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Sorry.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka xumahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I am very sorry.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am very sorry.” si ay u muujiso “aad ayaan uga xumahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “I apologize.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I apologize.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka raalli-gelinayaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I am sorry for being late.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am sorry for being late.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka xumahay daahitaanka”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I am sorry that I forgot.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am sorry that I forgot.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka xumahay inaan illoobay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I am sorry about the mistake.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am sorry about the mistake.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka xumahay khaladka”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Please accept my apology.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please accept my apology.” si ay u muujiso “fadlan aqbal raalli-gelintayda”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “It was my fault.” while apologizing for a small everyday mistake.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “It was my fault.” si ay u muujiso “anigaa khaladka lahaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: apologizing for a small everyday mistake • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Sorry.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Sorry..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I apologize.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I apologize..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I am sorry that I forgot.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I am sorry that I forgot..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan apologizing for a small everyday mistake. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan apologizing for a small everyday mistake. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Raalli-gelinta: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka sorry for + noun/verb-ing; apologize to + person + for + action, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Aqbalaadda raalli-gelinta waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Accepting an Apology.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “That is all right.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is all right.” si ay u muujiso “waa hagaag”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept apology. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waa hagaag iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “It is okay.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept apology. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waa hagaag iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept apology. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa dhib ma leh iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: accept apology. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ha ka welwelin iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I understand.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake, and her partner responded appropriately.

vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Please be more careful next time.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please be more careful next time.” si ay u muujiso “fadlan marka xigta taxaddar badan muuji”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: response. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan marka xigta taxaddar badan muuji iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Late arrival” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Late arrival”, oo macnihiisu yahay “imaansho daahay”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: minor mistake. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa imaansho daahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Missed appointment” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake and chose a suitable response.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: minor mistake. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ballan la illoobay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Wrong message” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake, so the meaning was clear.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: minor mistake. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fariin khaldan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: communication function. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aqbal raalli-gelin iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Accepting an Apology: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Aqbalaadda raalli-gelinta
grammar.explanationSomali: Choose an apology response that matches the seriousness of the situation. These examples concern only small everyday mistakes. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Choose an apology response that matches the seriousness of the situation. These examples concern only small everyday mistakes.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “That is all right.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is all right.” si ay u muujiso “waa hagaag”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “It is okay.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “It is okay.” si ay u muujiso “waa hagaag”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “No problem.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “No problem.” si ay u muujiso “dhib ma leh”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Do not worry about it.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do not worry about it.” si ay u muujiso “ha ka welwelin”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I understand.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I understand.” si ay u muujiso “waan fahmay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Thank you for apologizing.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thank you for apologizing.” si ay u muujiso “waad ku mahadsan tahay raalli-gelinta”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “These things happen.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “These things happen.” si ay u muujiso “waxyaalahan way dhacaan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Please be more careful next time.” while responding to an apology for a minor mistake.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please be more careful next time.” si ay u muujiso “fadlan marka xigta taxaddar badan muuji”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

practiceQuestions.question: Choose the accurate form for this Accepting an Apology situation (1).
options: A response with the wrong tone.
practiceQuestions.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “No problem.”. Function: Accepting an Apology. Tone-ka iyo context-ka ayaa jawaabtan ka dhigaya tan keliya ee ugu habboon. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg Accepting an Apology.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: responding to an apology for a minor mistake • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: That is all right.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: That is all right..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: No problem.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: No problem..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I understand.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I understand..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan responding to an apology for a minor mistake. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan responding to an apology for a minor mistake. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Aqbalaadda raalli-gelinta: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka apology + proportionate response, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Ammaanta iyo jawaabaha wanaagsan waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Compliments and Positive Responses.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “You did a great job.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You did a great job.” si ay u muujiso “shaqo wanaagsan ayaad qabatay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: compliment. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa shaqo wanaagsan ayaad qabatay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Your presentation was very clear.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Your presentation was very clear.” si ay u muujiso “bandhiggaagu aad buu u caddaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: compliment. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa bandhiggaagu aad buu u caddaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “That is a good idea.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is a good idea.” si ay u muujiso “taasi waa fikrad wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: compliment. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi waa fikrad wanaagsan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “You explained it very well.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You explained it very well.” si ay u muujiso “si fiican ayaad u sharaxday”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: compliment. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa si fiican ayaad u sharaxday iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I like your jacket.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I like your jacket.” si ay u muujiso “jaakaddaada waan ka helay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: compliment. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa jaakaddaada waan ka helay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: horumar aad u wanaagsan ayaad samaysay
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “You have made excellent progress.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You have made excellent progress.” si ay u muujiso “horumar aad u wanaagsan ayaad samaysay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: compliment. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa horumar aad u wanaagsan ayaad samaysay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “The food was delicious.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “The food was delicious.” si ay u muujiso “cuntadu aad bay u macaanayd”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: compliment. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa cuntadu aad bay u macaanayd iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “That is kind of you.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is kind of you.” si ay u muujiso “taasi naxariis ayay kaa tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: compliment response. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi naxariis ayay kaa tahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.pronunciation: i am glad you liked it.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I am glad you liked it.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill, and her partner responded appropriately.

vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I worked hard on it.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: response. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa si adag ayaan uga shaqeeyay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “I appreciate that.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill because it matched the situation.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: response. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taas waan uga mahadcelinayaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Positive feedback” in her notes while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill and used it correctly.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: compliment. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa jawaab-celin wanaagsan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Compliments and Positive Responses: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Ammaanta iyo jawaabaha wanaagsan
grammar.explanationSomali: A useful compliment is specific, respectful, and appropriate to the situation. Respond with thanks rather than rejecting the compliment. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: A useful compliment is specific, respectful, and appropriate to the situation. Respond with thanks rather than rejecting the compliment.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “You did a great job.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You did a great job.” si ay u muujiso “shaqo wanaagsan ayaad qabatay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Your presentation was very clear.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Your presentation was very clear.” si ay u muujiso “bandhiggaagu aad buu u caddaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “That is a good idea.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is a good idea.” si ay u muujiso “taasi waa fikrad wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “You explained it very well.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You explained it very well.” si ay u muujiso “si fiican ayaad u sharaxday”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I like your jacket.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I like your jacket.” si ay u muujiso “jaakaddaada waan ka helay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “You have made excellent progress.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You have made excellent progress.” si ay u muujiso “horumar aad u wanaagsan ayaad samaysay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “The food was delicious.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “The food was delicious.” si ay u muujiso “cuntadu aad bay u macaanayd”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “That is kind of you.” while complimenting a classmate on effort or skill.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is kind of you.” si ay u muujiso “taasi naxariis ayay kaa tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: complimenting a classmate on effort or skill • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: You did a great job.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: You did a great job..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: That is a good idea.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: That is a good idea..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I like your jacket.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I like your jacket..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan complimenting a classmate on effort or skill. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan complimenting a classmate on effort or skill. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Ammaanta iyo jawaabaha wanaagsan: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka specific compliment + appreciative response, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Dib-u-eegista casuumaadaha waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Invitations Review and Expansion.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Would you like to join the study group?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you like to join the study group?” si ay u muujiso “ma jeclaan lahayd inaad...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: invitation. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma jeclaan lahayd inaad...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Do you want to meet after class?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you want to meet after class?” si ay u muujiso “ma doonaysaa inaad...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: invitation. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma doonaysaa inaad...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ka warran inaan kulanno casharka ka dib?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “How about meeting after class?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “How about meeting after class?” si ay u muujiso “ka warran inaan kulanno casharka ka dib?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: invitation. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ka warran inaan kulanno casharka ka dib? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: What about joining the study group?
