vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “What about joining the study group?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What about joining the study group?” si ay u muujiso “ka warran inaad ku biirto kooxda waxbarashada?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: invitation. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ka warran inaad ku biirto kooxda waxbarashada? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Are you free tomorrow?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Are you free tomorrow?” si ay u muujiso “berri ma bannaan tahay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: invitation. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa berri ma bannaan tahay? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Shall we meet online?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Shall we meet online?” si ay u muujiso “ma ku kulannaa internetka?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: suggestion. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma ku kulannaa internetka? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Why do not we play football?
vocabulary.pronunciation: why do not we play football?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Why do not we play football?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Why do not we play football?” si ay u muujiso “maxaynu kubbad u ciyaari waynay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: suggestion. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa maxaynu kubbad u ciyaari waynay? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I would like to invite you to...
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: waxaan jeclaan lahaa inaan kugu casuumo...
vocabulary.pronunciation: i would like to invite you to
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I would like to invite you to our study group.” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I would like to invite you to our study group.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan jeclaan lahaa inaan kugu casuumo...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: invitation. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaan jeclaan lahaa inaan kugu casuumo... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Small gathering” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Small gathering”, oo macnihiisu yahay “kulan yar”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: event. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa kulan yar iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Would you be able to join us?
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: ma awoodi lahayd inaad nagu soo biirto?
vocabulary.pronunciation: would you be able to join us?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Would you be able to join us?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you be able to join us?” si ay u muujiso “ma awoodi lahayd inaad nagu soo biirto?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: invitation. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma awoodi lahayd inaad nagu soo biirto? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Please let me know.” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please let me know.” si ay u muujiso “fadlan ii soo sheeg”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request response. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan ii soo sheeg iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Community event” in her notes while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Community event”, oo macnihiisu yahay “munaasabad bulsho”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: event. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa munaasabad bulsho iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Invitations Review and Expansion: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Dib-u-eegista casuumaadaha
grammar.explanationSomali: Invitations can use would you like to, shall we, why do not we, or how about plus verb-ing. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Invitations can use would you like to, shall we, why do not we, or how about plus verb-ing.
grammar.sentenceStructure: Would you like to + verb?; How about + verb-ing?

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Would you like to join the study group?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would you like to join the study group?” si ay u muujiso “ma jeclaan lahayd inaad...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Do you want to meet after class?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you want to meet after class?” si ay u muujiso “ma doonaysaa inaad...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “How about meeting after class?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “How about meeting after class?” si ay u muujiso “ka warran inaan kulanno casharka ka dib?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “What about joining the study group?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What about joining the study group?” si ay u muujiso “ka warran inaad ku biirto kooxda waxbarashada?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Are you free tomorrow?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Are you free tomorrow?” si ay u muujiso “berri ma bannaan tahay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Shall we meet online?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Shall we meet online?” si ay u muujiso “ma ku kulannaa internetka?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Why do not we play football?” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Why do not we play football?” si ay u muujiso “maxaynu kubbad u ciyaari waynay?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I would like to invite you to our study group.” while inviting someone to a safe fictional activity.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I would like to invite you to our study group.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan jeclaan lahaa inaan kugu casuumo...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: inviting someone to a safe fictional activity • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Would you like to join the study group?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Would you like to join the study group?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: How about meeting after class?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: How about meeting after class?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Are you free tomorrow?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Are you free tomorrow?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan inviting someone to a safe fictional activity. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan inviting someone to a safe fictional activity. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Dib-u-eegista casuumaadaha: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka Would you like to + verb?; How about + verb-ing?, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.titleSomali: Aqbalaadda, diidmada iyo soo-jeedinta waqti kale
lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Aqbalaadda, diidmada iyo soo-jeedinta waqti kale waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Accepting, Declining and Suggesting Alternatives.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

grammar.titleEnglish: Accepting, Declining and Suggesting Alternatives: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Aqbalaadda, diidmada iyo soo-jeedinta waqti kale
grammar.explanationSomali: Accept warmly, decline politely, or suggest another time. A polite decline does not require a private explanation. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Accept warmly, decline politely, or suggest another time. A polite decline does not require a private explanation.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: responding to an invitation without sharing private details • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I would love to.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I would love to..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Thank you for inviting me.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Thank you for inviting me..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I would be happy to come.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I would be happy to come..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan responding to an invitation without sharing private details. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan responding to an invitation without sharing private details. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Aqbalaadda, diidmada iyo soo-jeedinta waqti kale: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka thanks + accept/decline; alternative time + instead, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Talo weydiisashada waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Asking for Advice.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “What should I do?” while asking for advice about a study plan, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What should I do?” si ay u muujiso “maxaan sameeyaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ask advice. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa maxaan sameeyaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: What do you think I should do?
vocabulary.pronunciation: what do you think i should do?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “What do you think I should do?” while asking for advice about a study plan.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What do you think I should do?” si ay u muujiso “maxaad u malaynaysaa inaan sameeyo?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ask advice. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa maxaad u malaynaysaa inaan sameeyo? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Can you give me some advice?
vocabulary.pronunciation: can you give me some advice?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Can you give me some advice?” while asking for advice about a study plan because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can you give me some advice?” si ay u muujiso “talo ma i siin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ask advice. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa talo ma i siin kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Do you have any suggestions?” while asking for advice about a study plan.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you have any suggestions?” si ay u muujiso “wax soo-jeedin ah ma haysaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ask advice. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa wax soo-jeedin ah ma haysaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Which option do you recommend?” while asking for advice about a study plan, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Which option do you recommend?” si ay u muujiso “ikhtiyaarkee ayaad ku talinaysaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ask advice. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ikhtiyaarkee ayaad ku talinaysaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Would it be better to?” while asking for advice about a study plan.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would it be better to?” si ay u muujiso “ma ka fiicnaan lahayd inaan...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ask advice. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma ka fiicnaan lahayd inaan...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “What would you do?” while asking for advice about a study plan because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What would you do?” si ay u muujiso “adigu maxaad samayn lahayd?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ask advice. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa adigu maxaad samayn lahayd? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Some advice” in her notes while asking for advice about a study plan and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Some advice”, oo macnihiisu yahay “talo”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: advice noun. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa talo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “A piece of advice” while asking for advice about a study plan, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “A piece of advice”, oo macnihiisu yahay “hal talo”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: advice noun. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa hal talo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The teacher advised us to review the difficult lesson before the quiz.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Macallinku wuxuu nagula taliyay inaan casharka adag dib u eegno quiz-ka ka hor.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: verb. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa talo sii iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Recommend” while asking for advice about a study plan, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Recommend”, oo macnihiisu yahay “ku tali”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: verb. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ku tali iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Suggestion” in her notes while asking for advice about a study plan and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Suggestion”, oo macnihiisu yahay “soo-jeedin”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: noun. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa soo-jeedin iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Asking for Advice: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Talo weydiisashada
grammar.explanationSomali: Advice is an uncountable noun; say some advice or a piece of advice, never an advice. Advise is the verb. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Advice is an uncountable noun; say some advice or a piece of advice, never an advice. Advise is the verb.
grammar.sentenceStructure: ask for advice; ask what someone recommends

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “What should I do?” while asking for advice about a study plan, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What should I do?” si ay u muujiso “maxaan sameeyaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Can you give me some advice?” while asking for advice about a study plan because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can you give me some advice?” si ay u muujiso “talo ma i siin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Do you have any suggestions?” while asking for advice about a study plan.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you have any suggestions?” si ay u muujiso “wax soo-jeedin ah ma haysaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Which option do you recommend?” while asking for advice about a study plan, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Which option do you recommend?” si ay u muujiso “ikhtiyaarkee ayaad ku talinaysaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Would it be better to?” while asking for advice about a study plan.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Would it be better to?” si ay u muujiso “ma ka fiicnaan lahayd inaan...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “What would you do?” while asking for advice about a study plan because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What would you do?” si ay u muujiso “adigu maxaad samayn lahayd?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Hodan recorded “Some advice” in her notes while asking for advice about a study plan and used it correctly.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Some advice”, oo macnihiisu yahay “talo”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: asking for advice about a study plan • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What should I do?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: What should I do?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Can you give me some advice?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Can you give me some advice?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Which option do you recommend?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Which option do you recommend?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan asking for advice about a study plan. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan asking for advice about a study plan. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Talo weydiisashada: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka ask for advice; ask what someone recommends, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Talo bixinta waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Giving Advice.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: waa inaad casharka dib u eegtaa
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “You should review the lesson.” while giving gentle advice about studying, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You should review the lesson.” si ay u muujiso “waa inaad casharka dib u eegtaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: advice. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waa inaad casharka dib u eegtaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “You should not rush.” while giving gentle advice about studying.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You should not rush.” si ay u muujiso “waa inaadan degdegin”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: advice. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waa inaadan degdegin iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “You could ask the adviser.” while giving gentle advice about studying because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You could ask the adviser.” si ay u muujiso “waxaad weydiin kartaa lataliyaha”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: suggestion. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaad weydiin kartaa lataliyaha iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Why do not you make a plan?
vocabulary.pronunciation: why do not you make a plan?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Why do not you make a plan?” while giving gentle advice about studying.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: advice. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa maxaad qorshe u samayn wayday? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.pronunciation: it might be a good idea to confirm.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “It might be a good idea to confirm.” while giving gentle advice about studying, and her partner responded appropriately.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: suggestion. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaa laga yaabaa inay fiicnaato inaad xaqiijiso iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.

grammar.titleEnglish: Giving Advice: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Talo bixinta
grammar.explanationSomali: Should gives clear advice. Could and might offer softer possibilities. If I were you is learned as one useful phrase, not a full conditional lesson. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Should gives clear advice. Could and might offer softer possibilities. If I were you is learned as one useful phrase, not a full conditional lesson.
grammar.sentenceStructure: You should + base verb; You could/might + base verb

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “You should review the lesson.” while giving gentle advice about studying, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You should review the lesson.” si ay u muujiso “waa inaad casharka dib u eegtaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: giving gentle advice about studying • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: You should review the lesson.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: You should review the lesson..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: You could ask the adviser.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: You could ask the adviser..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: It might be a good idea to confirm.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: It might be a good idea to confirm..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 2: response iyo follow-up • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: You should review the lesson.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: You should review the lesson..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: You could ask the adviser.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: You could ask the adviser..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: It might be a good idea to confirm.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan giving gentle advice about studying. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan giving gentle advice about studying. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Talo bixinta: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka You should + base verb; You could/might + base verb, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Heshiinta waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Agreeing.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I agree.” while agreeing during a simple group decision, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I agree.” si ay u muujiso “waan ku raacsanahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: agreement. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ku raacsanahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “You are right.” while agreeing during a simple group decision.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “You are right.” si ay u muujiso “waad saxan tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: agreement. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waad saxan tahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “That is true.” while agreeing during a simple group decision because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is true.” si ay u muujiso “taasi waa run”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: agreement. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi waa run iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Exactly.” while agreeing during a simple group decision.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Exactly.” si ay u muujiso “sidaas ayay tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: agreement. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa sidaas ayay tahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I think so too.” while agreeing during a simple group decision, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I think so too.” si ay u muujiso “aniguna sidaas ayaan u malaynayaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: agreement. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aniguna sidaas ayaan u malaynayaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I completely agree.” while agreeing during a simple group decision.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I completely agree.” si ay u muujiso “si buuxda ayaan u raacsanahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: agreement. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa si buuxda ayaan u raacsanahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: agreement. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi waa qodob wanaagsan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Convenient option” while agreeing during a simple group decision, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Convenient option”, oo macnihiisu yahay “ikhtiyaar habboon”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: opinion. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ikhtiyaar habboon iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Confirm the time” while agreeing during a simple group decision and chose a suitable response.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Confirm the time”, oo macnihiisu yahay “xaqiiji waqtiga”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: decision. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa xaqiiji waqtiga iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Same opinion” while agreeing during a simple group decision, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Same opinion”, oo macnihiisu yahay “isku aragti”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: agreement. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa isku aragti iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Agree with someone” in her notes while agreeing during a simple group decision and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Agree with someone”, oo macnihiisu yahay “qof ku raac”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: collocation. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa qof ku raac iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Agreeing: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Heshiinta
grammar.explanationSomali: Agree is a verb, so say I agree, not I am agree. Give a reason when it helps the discussion. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Agree is a verb, so say I agree, not I am agree. Give a reason when it helps the discussion.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I agree.” while agreeing during a simple group decision, and her partner responded appropriately.

positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I think so too.” si ay u muujiso “aniguna sidaas ayaan u malaynayaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is a good point.” si ay u muujiso “taasi waa qodob wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: agreeing during a simple group decision • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I agree.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I agree..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: That is true.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: That is true..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I think so too.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I think so too.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I think so too..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan agreeing during a simple group decision. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan agreeing during a simple group decision. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Heshiinta: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka I agree + that-clause; agreement + reason, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Khilaafka edebta leh waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Disagreeing Politely.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.englishWord: I am not sure I agree.
vocabulary.pronunciation: i am not sure i agree.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I am not sure I agree.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am not sure I agree.” si ay u muujiso “ma hubo inaan ku raacsanahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: disagreement. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma hubo inaan ku raacsanahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I see your point, but I prefer the earlier time.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I see your point, but I prefer the earlier time.” si ay u muujiso “qodobkaaga waan fahmay, laakiin...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: disagreement. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa qodobkaaga waan fahmay, laakiin... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: taasi run way noqon kartaa, laakiin...
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hassan said, “That may be true, but the other route is shorter.” during the discussion.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Hassan wuxuu wada-hadalka ku yiri: “Taasi run way noqon kartaa, laakiin jidka kale ayaa gaaban.”
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: disagreement. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi run way noqon kartaa, laakiin... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I understand what you mean, however...
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: waan fahmay waxaad ula jeeddo, hase yeeshee...
vocabulary.pronunciation: i understand what you mean, however
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I understand what you mean; however, we need more time.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I understand what you mean; however, we need more time.” si ay u muujiso “waan fahmay waxaad ula jeeddo, hase yeeshee...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: disagreement. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan fahmay waxaad ula jeeddo, hase yeeshee... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I have a different opinion.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I have a different opinion.” si ay u muujiso “aragti kale ayaan qabaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: disagreement. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aragti kale ayaan qabaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I do not think that is the best option.
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: uma malaynayo inay tahay ikhtiyaarka ugu fiican
vocabulary.pronunciation: i do not think that is the best option.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I do not think that is the best option.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I do not think that is the best option.” si ay u muujiso “uma malaynayo inay tahay ikhtiyaarka ugu fiican”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: disagreement. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa uma malaynayo inay tahay ikhtiyaarka ugu fiican iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Perhaps we should consider another idea.
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: malaha waa inaan fikrad kale tixgelinnaa
vocabulary.pronunciation: perhaps we should consider another idea.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Perhaps we should consider another idea.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Perhaps we should consider another idea.” si ay u muujiso “malaha waa inaan fikrad kale tixgelinnaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: alternative. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa malaha waa inaan fikrad kale tixgelinnaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I do not think that is correct.
vocabulary.pronunciation: i do not think that is correct.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I do not think that is correct.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I do not think that is correct.” si ay u muujiso “uma malaynayo inay sax tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: correction. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa uma malaynayo inay sax tahay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Best route” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Best route”, oo macnihiisu yahay “jidka ugu fiican”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: opinion topic. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa jidka ugu fiican iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Course schedule” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route and chose a suitable response.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Course schedule”, oo macnihiisu yahay “jadwalka koorsada”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: opinion topic. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa jadwalka koorsada iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Different opinion” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Different opinion”, oo macnihiisu yahay “aragti kale”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: disagreement. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aragti kale iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Consider another idea” in her notes while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Consider another idea”, oo macnihiisu yahay “tixgeli fikrad kale”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: alternative. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa tixgeli fikrad kale iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Disagreeing Politely: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Khilaafka edebta leh
grammar.explanationSomali: Acknowledge the other view before giving a different opinion. Avoid the direct phrase you are wrong when a softer correction is suitable. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Acknowledge the other view before giving a different opinion. Avoid the direct phrase you are wrong when a softer correction is suitable.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I am not sure I agree.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am not sure I agree.” si ay u muujiso “ma hubo inaan ku raacsanahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I see your point, but I prefer the earlier time.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I see your point, but I prefer the earlier time.” si ay u muujiso “qodobkaaga waan fahmay, laakiin...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Hassan said, “That may be true, but the other route is shorter.” during the discussion.
positiveExamples.somali: Hassan wuxuu wada-hadalka ku yiri: “Taasi run way noqon kartaa, laakiin jidka kale ayaa gaaban.”

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I understand what you mean; however, we need more time.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I understand what you mean; however, we need more time.” si ay u muujiso “waan fahmay waxaad ula jeeddo, hase yeeshee...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I have a different opinion.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I have a different opinion.” si ay u muujiso “aragti kale ayaan qabaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I do not think that is the best option.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I do not think that is the best option.” si ay u muujiso “uma malaynayo inay tahay ikhtiyaarka ugu fiican”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Perhaps we should consider another idea.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Perhaps we should consider another idea.” si ay u muujiso “malaha waa inaan fikrad kale tixgelinnaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I do not think that is correct.” while disagreeing about a simple schedule or route.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I do not think that is correct.” si ay u muujiso “uma malaynayo inay sax tahay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: disagreeing about a simple schedule or route • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I am not sure I agree.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I am not sure I agree..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: That may be true, but the other route is shorter.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: That may be true, but the other route is shorter..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I have a different opinion.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I have a different opinion..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan disagreeing about a simple schedule or route. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan disagreeing about a simple schedule or route. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Khilaafka edebta leh: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka acknowledge point + but/however + different view, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Caddeynta iyo hubinta fahamka waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Clarifying and Checking Understanding.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “What do you mean?” while checking an unclear meeting detail, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What do you mean?” si ay u muujiso “maxaad ula jeeddaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: clarification. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa maxaad ula jeeddaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Could you explain that?” while checking an unclear meeting detail.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could you explain that?” si ay u muujiso “taas ma sharxi kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: clarification. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taas ma sharxi kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Do you mean the meeting starts at three?” while checking an unclear meeting detail because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Do you mean the meeting starts at three?” si ay u muujiso “ma waxaad ula jeeddaa...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: clarification. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma waxaad ula jeeddaa...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Are you saying that the class is on Thursday?” while checking an unclear meeting detail.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Are you saying that the class is on Thursday?” si ay u muujiso “ma waxaad sheegaysaa in...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: clarification. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma waxaad sheegaysaa in...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Let me check that I understood.
vocabulary.pronunciation: let me check that i understood.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Let me check that I understood.” while checking an unclear meeting detail, and her partner responded appropriately.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: checking. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aan hubiyo inaan fahmay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: checking. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa kulanku saddexda ayuu dhacayaa, sax? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Did you say Tuesday or Thursday?
vocabulary.pronunciation: did you say tuesday or thursday?

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: clarification. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa mar kale ma oran kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: clarification. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma higgaadin kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Is that correct?” si ay u muujiso “taasi ma sax baa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: checking. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taasi ma sax baa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “That is what I mean.” while checking an unclear meeting detail because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “That is what I mean.” si ay u muujiso “taas ayaan ula jeedaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: confirm. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa taas ayaan ula jeedaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Let me explain it another way.
vocabulary.pronunciation: let me explain it another way.

vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Let me explain it another way.” si ay u muujiso “aan si kale u sharaxo”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: clarification. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aan si kale u sharaxo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Clarifying and Checking Understanding: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Caddeynta iyo hubinta fahamka
grammar.explanationSomali: Use a polite clarification question, then repeat the detail in your own words to check understanding. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Use a polite clarification question, then repeat the detail in your own words to check understanding.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “What do you mean?” while checking an unclear meeting detail, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “What do you mean?” si ay u muujiso “maxaad ula jeeddaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “The meeting is at three, right?” si ay u muujiso “kulanku saddexda ayuu dhacayaa, sax?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

practiceQuestions.question: Choose the accurate form for this Clarifying and Checking Understanding situation (1).
options: Do you mean the meeting starts at three?
A response with the wrong tone.
practiceQuestions.correctAnswer: Do you mean the meeting starts at three?
practiceQuestions.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Do you mean the meeting starts at three?”. Function: Clarifying and Checking Understanding. Tone-ka iyo context-ka ayaa jawaabtan ka dhigaya tan keliya ee ugu habboon. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg Clarifying and Checking Understanding.

options: Are you saying that the class is on Thursday?

examples.english: The trainer chose “Do you mean the meeting starts at three?” while checking an unclear meeting detail because it matched the situation.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: checking an unclear meeting detail • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What do you mean?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: What do you mean?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Do you mean the meeting starts at three?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Do you mean the meeting starts at three?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Let me check that I understood.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Let me check that I understood..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan checking an unclear meeting detail. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan checking an unclear meeting detail. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Caddeynta iyo hubinta fahamka: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka clarification question + confirmed detail, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.
