lessons.titleSomali: Hadalka si edeb leh uga dhex-gelidda
lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Hadalka si edeb leh uga dhex-gelidda waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Interrupting Politely.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Excuse me for interrupting.” while asking a quick question during a group discussion, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Excuse me for interrupting.” si ay u muujiso “iga raalli noqo hadal dhex-gelinta”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: interruption. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa iga raalli noqo hadal dhex-gelinta iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: waan ka xumahay inaan dhex galo, laakiin...
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Sorry to interrupt, but could I ask a quick question?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Sorry to interrupt, but could I ask a quick question?” si ay u muujiso “waan ka xumahay inaan dhex galo, laakiin...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: interruption. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ka xumahay inaan dhex galo, laakiin... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “May I add something?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I add something?” si ay u muujiso “wax ma ku dari karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: interruption. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa wax ma ku dari karaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: halkan wax ma ka oran karaa?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Can I say something here?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can I say something here?” si ay u muujiso “halkan wax ma ka oran karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: interruption. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa halkan wax ma ka oran karaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Could I ask a quick question?
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: su’aal degdeg ah ma weydiin karaa?
vocabulary.pronunciation: could i ask a quick question?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Could I ask a quick question?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could I ask a quick question?” si ay u muujiso “su’aal degdeg ah ma weydiin karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: interruption. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa su’aal degdeg ah ma weydiin karaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “May I clarify one point?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I clarify one point?” si ay u muujiso “hal qodob ma caddayn karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: clarification. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa hal qodob ma caddayn karaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Wait for a pause” while asking a quick question during a group discussion, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Wait for a pause”, oo macnihiisu yahay “sug hakad”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: conversation skill. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa sug hakad iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Group discussion” in her notes while asking a quick question during a group discussion and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Group discussion”, oo macnihiisu yahay “wada-hadal kooxeed”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: context. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa wada-hadal kooxeed iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Quick question” while asking a quick question during a group discussion, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Quick question”, oo macnihiisu yahay “su’aal degdeg ah”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: interruption. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa su’aal degdeg ah iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Before we continue” while asking a quick question during a group discussion and chose a suitable response.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Before we continue”, oo macnihiisu yahay “inta aynaan sii wadin”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: transition. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa inta aynaan sii wadin iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Suitable moment” while asking a quick question during a group discussion, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Suitable moment”, oo macnihiisu yahay “waqti ku habboon”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: conversation skill. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waqti ku habboon iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: si edeb leh u dhex gal
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Please wait for a short pause so you can interrupt politely.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Fadlan sug hakad gaaban si aad hadalka si edeb leh uga dhex gasho.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: communication function. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa si edeb leh u dhex gal iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Interrupting Politely: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Hadalka si edeb leh uga dhex-gelidda
grammar.explanationSomali: Wait for a suitable pause when possible, apologize briefly, and state why the interruption is necessary. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Wait for a suitable pause when possible, apologize briefly, and state why the interruption is necessary.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

examples.english: Amina said, “Excuse me for interrupting.” while asking a quick question during a group discussion, and her partner responded appropriately.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Excuse me for interrupting.” si ay u muujiso “iga raalli noqo hadal dhex-gelinta”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Sorry to interrupt, but could I ask a quick question?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion.

examples.english: The trainer chose “May I add something?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion because it matched the situation.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I add something?” si ay u muujiso “wax ma ku dari karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Can I say something here?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can I say something here?” si ay u muujiso “halkan wax ma ka oran karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: Amina said, “Could I ask a quick question?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion, and her partner responded appropriately.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could I ask a quick question?” si ay u muujiso “su’aal degdeg ah ma weydiin karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “May I clarify one point?” while asking a quick question during a group discussion.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I clarify one point?” si ay u muujiso “hal qodob ma caddayn karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: The role-play included “Wait for a pause” while asking a quick question during a group discussion, so the meaning was clear.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Wait for a pause”, oo macnihiisu yahay “sug hakad”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: asking a quick question during a group discussion • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Excuse me for interrupting.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Excuse me for interrupting..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: May I add something?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: May I add something?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Could I ask a quick question?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Could I ask a quick question?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan asking a quick question during a group discussion. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan asking a quick question during a group discussion. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Hadalka si edeb leh uga dhex-gelidda: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka brief apology + reason/question, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Wada sheekeysiga telefoonka waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Telephone Conversations.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Hello, this is” while handling a fictional telephone call, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Hello, this is” si ay u muujiso “hello, waxaa kula hadlaya...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone opening. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa hello, waxaa kula hadlaya... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “May I speak to?” while handling a fictional telephone call.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I speak to?” si ay u muujiso “ma la hadli karaa...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone request. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa ma la hadli karaa...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Who is calling, please?” while handling a fictional telephone call because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Who is calling, please?” si ay u muujiso “yaa soo wacaya, fadlan?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone question. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa yaa soo wacaya, fadlan? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Please hold.” while handling a fictional telephone call.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please hold.” si ay u muujiso “fadlan khadka ku sug”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone instruction. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan khadka ku sug iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I will put you through.” while handling a fictional telephone call, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I will put you through.” si ay u muujiso “waan kugu xirayaa qofka”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone response. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan kugu xirayaa qofka iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “The line is busy.” while handling a fictional telephone call.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “The line is busy.” si ay u muujiso “khadku waa mashquul”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone information. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa khadku waa mashquul iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “She is not available.” while handling a fictional telephone call because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “She is not available.” si ay u muujiso “hadda lama heli karo”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone information. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa hadda lama heli karo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Can I take a message?” while handling a fictional telephone call.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can I take a message?” si ay u muujiso “fariin ma kaa qaadi karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone offer. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fariin ma kaa qaadi karaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Please call me back.” while handling a fictional telephone call, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please call me back.” si ay u muujiso “fadlan dib ii soo wac”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: callback request. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan dib ii soo wac iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “We were disconnected.” while handling a fictional telephone call.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “We were disconnected.” si ay u muujiso “khadku naga go’ay”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone problem. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa khadku naga go’ay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “The line is not clear.” while handling a fictional telephone call because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “The line is not clear.” si ay u muujiso “khadku ma cadda”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone problem. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa khadku ma cadda iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Wrong number” in her notes while handling a fictional telephone call and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Wrong number”, oo macnihiisu yahay “lambar khaldan”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: phone problem. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa lambar khaldan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Telephone Conversations: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Wada sheekeysiga telefoonka
grammar.explanationSomali: Identify yourself, ask for the person, clarify poor sound, and close the call politely. No real numbers are used. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Identify yourself, ask for the person, clarify poor sound, and close the call politely. No real numbers are used.

positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please hold.” si ay u muujiso “fadlan khadka ku sug”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I will put you through.” while handling a fictional telephone call, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I will put you through.” si ay u muujiso “waan kugu xirayaa qofka”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “The line is busy.” while handling a fictional telephone call.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “The line is busy.” si ay u muujiso “khadku waa mashquul”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “She is not available.” while handling a fictional telephone call because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “She is not available.” si ay u muujiso “hadda lama heli karo”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Can I take a message?” while handling a fictional telephone call.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

lines.english: Hello, this is

lines.english: Who is calling, please?

lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan handling a fictional telephone call. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan handling a fictional telephone call. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Wada sheekeysiga telefoonka: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka greeting + identity + purpose + response + closing, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Qaadista iyo reebista fariimaha waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Taking and Leaving Messages.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Can I leave a message?” while recording a fictional callback message, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can I leave a message?” si ay u muujiso “fariin ma reebi karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: leave message. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fariin ma reebi karaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Please tell her that the meeting starts at four.” while recording a fictional callback message.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please tell her that the meeting starts at four.” si ay u muujiso “fadlan u sheeg inay...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: message detail. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan u sheeg inay... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: Ask him to call me back.
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: weydii inuu dib ii soo waco
vocabulary.pronunciation: ask him to call me back.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Ask him to call me back.” while recording a fictional callback message because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Ask him to call me back.” si ay u muujiso “weydii inuu dib ii soo waco”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: callback. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa weydii inuu dib ii soo waco iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “May I have your name?” while recording a fictional callback message.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I have your name?” si ay u muujiso “magacaaga ma heli karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: message detail. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa magacaaga ma heli karaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Can you repeat the number?” while recording a fictional callback message, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can you repeat the number?” si ay u muujiso “lambarka ma ku celin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: clarification. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa lambarka ma ku celin kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I will make sure she gets the message.
vocabulary.pronunciation: i will make sure she gets the message.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I will make sure she gets the message.” while recording a fictional callback message.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I will make sure she gets the message.” si ay u muujiso “waan hubinayaa inay fariinta hesho”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: message promise. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan hubinayaa inay fariinta hesho iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Caller name” while recording a fictional callback message, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Caller name”, oo macnihiisu yahay “magaca soo wacaha”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: message field. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa magaca soo wacaha iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Person requested” in her notes while recording a fictional callback message and used it correctly.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: message field. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa qofka la doonayo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Reason for calling” while recording a fictional callback message, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Reason for calling”, oo macnihiisu yahay “sababta wicitaanka”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: message field. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa sababta wicitaanka iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Callback request” while recording a fictional callback message and chose a suitable response.

grammar.titleEnglish: Taking and Leaving Messages: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Qaadista iyo reebista fariimaha
grammar.explanationSomali: A complete phone message records the caller, requested person, reason, callback details, time, and general urgency. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: A complete phone message records the caller, requested person, reason, callback details, time, and general urgency.
grammar.sentenceStructure: caller + requested person + reason + callback + time

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Can I leave a message?” while recording a fictional callback message, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can I leave a message?” si ay u muujiso “fariin ma reebi karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Please tell her that the meeting starts at four.” while recording a fictional callback message.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Please tell her that the meeting starts at four.” si ay u muujiso “fadlan u sheeg inay...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “Ask him to call me back.” while recording a fictional callback message because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Ask him to call me back.” si ay u muujiso “weydii inuu dib ii soo waco”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “May I have your name?” while recording a fictional callback message.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “May I have your name?” si ay u muujiso “magacaaga ma heli karaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “Can you repeat the number?” while recording a fictional callback message, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Can you repeat the number?” si ay u muujiso “lambarka ma ku celin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I will make sure she gets the message.” while recording a fictional callback message.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I will make sure she gets the message.” si ay u muujiso “waan hubinayaa inay fariinta hesho”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The role-play included “Caller name” while recording a fictional callback message, so the meaning was clear.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Caller name”, oo macnihiisu yahay “magaca soo wacaha”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.

positiveExamples.english: Hodan recorded “Person requested” in her notes while recording a fictional callback message and used it correctly.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Person requested”, oo macnihiisu yahay “qofka la doonayo”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

practiceQuestions.question: Choose the accurate form for this Taking and Leaving Messages situation (1).
options: Ask him to call me back.
A response with the wrong tone.
practiceQuestions.correctAnswer: Ask him to call me back.
practiceQuestions.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Ask him to call me back.”. Function: Taking and Leaving Messages. Tone-ka iyo context-ka ayaa jawaabtan ka dhigaya tan keliya ee ugu habboon. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg Taking and Leaving Messages.

dialogues.titleSomali: Mock phone-message form 1: caller, person, reason, callback, time, urgency • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Can I leave a message?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Can I leave a message?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Ask him to call me back.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Ask him to call me back..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Can you repeat the number?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Can you repeat the number?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Caller name
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Caller name.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan recording a fictional callback message. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan recording a fictional callback message. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Qaadista iyo reebista fariimaha: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka caller + requested person + reason + callback + time, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Fariimaha qoraalka iyo chat-ka waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Text and Chat Messages.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Confirm a plan” while sending a clear fictional chat message, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Confirm a plan”, oo macnihiisu yahay “xaqiiji qorshe”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: message purpose. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa xaqiiji qorshe iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Text and Chat Messages: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Fariimaha qoraalka iyo chat-ka
grammar.explanationSomali: A clear message has a greeting, reason, important detail, requested action, and suitable closing. Match tone to the reader. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: A clear message has a greeting, reason, important detail, requested action, and suitable closing. Match tone to the reader.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan sending a clear fictional chat message. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan sending a clear fictional chat message. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Fariimaha qoraalka iyo chat-ka: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka greeting + reason + detail/action + closing, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.titleSomali: Xiriirka rasmiga ah iyo kan aan rasmiga ahayn
lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Xiriirka rasmiga ah iyo kan aan rasmiga ahayn waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Formal and Informal Communication.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Formal” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Formal”, oo macnihiisu yahay “rasmi”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: tone. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa rasmi iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Neutral” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener and chose a suitable response.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Neutral”, oo macnihiisu yahay “dhexdhexaad”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: tone. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa dhexdhexaad iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Informal” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Informal” si ay u muujiso “aan rasmi ahayn”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: tone. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aan rasmi ahayn iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Good morning.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Good morning.” si ay u muujiso “subax wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: greeting. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa subax wanaagsan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Could you please assist me?” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could you please assist me?” si ay u muujiso “fadlan ma i caawin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan ma i caawin kartaa? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Thank you for your help.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thank you for your help.” si ay u muujiso “waad ku mahadsan tahay caawimadaada”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waad ku mahadsan tahay caawimadaada iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “I apologize for arriving late.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I apologize for arriving late.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka raalli-gelinayaa daahitaanka”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: apology. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan ka raalli-gelinayaa daahitaanka iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I look forward to hearing from you.
vocabulary.pronunciation: i look forward to hearing from you.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I look forward to hearing from you.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I look forward to hearing from you.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan sugayaa jawaabtaada”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: closing. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaan sugayaa jawaabtaada iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Hi.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Hi.” si ay u muujiso “iska warran”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: greeting. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa iska warran iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Thanks.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thanks.” si ay u muujiso “mahadsanid”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: thanks. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa mahadsanid iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “See you later.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “See you later.” si ay u muujiso “gadaal ayaan is arki doonnaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ending. Tone: informal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa gadaal ayaan is arki doonnaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Hodan recorded “Unknown person” in her notes while choosing a suitable tone for the listener and used it correctly.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Unknown person”, oo macnihiisu yahay “qof aan la aqoon”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: audience. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa qof aan la aqoon iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Formal and Informal Communication: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Xiriirka rasmiga ah iyo kan aan rasmiga ahayn
grammar.explanationSomali: Formal, neutral, and informal language depend on relationship and situation. Polite does not always mean highly formal. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Formal, neutral, and informal language depend on relationship and situation. Polite does not always mean highly formal.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

examples.english: The trainer explained “Formal” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener, then showed its natural collocation.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Formal”, oo macnihiisu yahay “rasmi”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.

examples.english: Learners practised the term “Neutral” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener and chose a suitable response.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Neutral”, oo macnihiisu yahay “dhexdhexaad”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.

examples.english: The trainer chose “Informal” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener because it matched the situation.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Informal” si ay u muujiso “aan rasmi ahayn”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Good morning.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Good morning.” si ay u muujiso “subax wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: Amina said, “Could you please assist me?” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener, and her partner responded appropriately.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could you please assist me?” si ay u muujiso “fadlan ma i caawin kartaa?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Thank you for your help.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thank you for your help.” si ay u muujiso “waad ku mahadsan tahay caawimadaada”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: The trainer chose “I apologize for arriving late.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener because it matched the situation.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I apologize for arriving late.” si ay u muujiso “waan ka raalli-gelinayaa daahitaanka”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I look forward to hearing from you.” while choosing a suitable tone for the listener.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I look forward to hearing from you.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan sugayaa jawaabtaada”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan choosing a suitable tone for the listener. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan choosing a suitable tone for the listener. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Xiriirka rasmiga ah iyo kan aan rasmiga ahayn: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka relationship + situation -> suitable tone, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Dhammeystirka wada sheekeysiga waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Ending a Conversation.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.englishWord: It was nice talking to you.
vocabulary.pronunciation: it was nice talking to you.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “It was nice talking to you.” while ending a conversation naturally, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “It was nice talking to you.” si ay u muujiso “way fiicnayd inaan kula hadlo”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ending. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa way fiicnayd inaan kula hadlo iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I should let you get back to work.
vocabulary.somaliMeaning: waa inaan kuu daayaa inaad shaqada ku noqoto
vocabulary.pronunciation: i should let you get back to work.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I should let you get back to work.” while ending a conversation naturally.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I should let you get back to work.” si ay u muujiso “waa inaan kuu daayaa inaad shaqada ku noqoto”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ending. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waa inaan kuu daayaa inaad shaqada ku noqoto iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I am afraid I have to go.
vocabulary.pronunciation: i am afraid i have to go.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “I am afraid I have to go.” while ending a conversation naturally because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am afraid I have to go.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan ka xumahay inaan tago”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ending. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaan ka xumahay inaan tago iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Thank you for your time.” while ending a conversation naturally.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thank you for your time.” si ay u muujiso “waad ku mahadsan tahay waqtigaaga”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ending. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waad ku mahadsan tahay waqtigaaga iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.englishWord: I will speak to you soon.
vocabulary.pronunciation: i will speak to you soon.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “I will speak to you soon.” while ending a conversation naturally, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I will speak to you soon.” si ay u muujiso “dhowaan ayaan kula hadli doonaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ending. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa dhowaan ayaan kula hadli doonaa iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: ending. Tone: friendly. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa aan xiriirka sii wadno iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.titleEnglish: Ending a Conversation: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Dhammeystirka wada sheekeysiga
grammar.explanationSomali: Signal the ending, complete any final practical detail, and choose a closing that fits the relationship. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Signal the ending, complete any final practical detail, and choose a closing that fits the relationship.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “It was nice talking to you.” while ending a conversation naturally, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “It was nice talking to you.” si ay u muujiso “way fiicnayd inaan kula hadlo”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I should let you get back to work.” while ending a conversation naturally.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I should let you get back to work.” si ay u muujiso “waa inaan kuu daayaa inaad shaqada ku noqoto”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “I am afraid I have to go.” while ending a conversation naturally because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I am afraid I have to go.” si ay u muujiso “waxaan ka xumahay inaan tago”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Thank you for your time.” while ending a conversation naturally.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Thank you for your time.” si ay u muujiso “waad ku mahadsan tahay waqtigaaga”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: Amina said, “I will speak to you soon.” while ending a conversation naturally, and her partner responded appropriately.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I will speak to you soon.” si ay u muujiso “dhowaan ayaan kula hadli doonaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Let us keep in touch.” while ending a conversation naturally.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Let us keep in touch.” si ay u muujiso “aan xiriirka sii wadno”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: The trainer chose “See you tomorrow.” while ending a conversation naturally because it matched the situation.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “See you tomorrow.” si ay u muujiso “berri ayaan is arki doonnaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

positiveExamples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Have a good day.” while ending a conversation naturally.
positiveExamples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Have a good day.” si ay u muujiso “maalin wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a private question when safe small talk is more appropriate.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran su’aal gaar ah marka wada-hadal ammaan ahi ku habboon yahay.

negativeExamples.english: The speaker did not use an informal phrase with an unknown customer.
negativeExamples.somali: Qofkii hadlayay uma adeegsan qof macmiil aan la aqoon weedh aan rasmi ahayn.

questionExamples.english: Could you tell me which response is most appropriate here?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa jawaabta halkan ugu habboon?

questionExamples.english: Do you know whether this phrase is formal or informal?
questionExamples.somali: Ma ogtahay in weedhani rasmi tahay ama aan rasmi ahayn?

commonMistakesSomali: Ha isku darin qaababka verb-ka iyo preposition-ka.
Ha dooran response wanaagsan ama xun adigoon context-ka eegin.
Advice waa uncountable; ha oran an advice.
Tell qof ayuu qaataa; explain-na waxaa la yiraahdaa explain something to someone.

practiceQuestions.question: Choose the accurate form for this Ending a Conversation situation (1).
options: I am afraid I have to go.
A response with the wrong tone.
practiceQuestions.correctAnswer: I am afraid I have to go.

examples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “I should let you get back to work.” while ending a conversation naturally.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I should let you get back to work.” si ay u muujiso “waa inaan kuu daayaa inaad shaqada ku noqoto”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: The trainer chose “I am afraid I have to go.” while ending a conversation naturally because it matched the situation.

examples.english: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “Thank you for your time.” while ending a conversation naturally.

examples.english: Amina said, “I will speak to you soon.” while ending a conversation naturally, and her partner responded appropriately.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I will speak to you soon.” si ay u muujiso “dhowaan ayaan kula hadli doonaa”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

examples.english: During a practice conversation, Hassan said, “Let us keep in touch.” while ending a conversation naturally.
examples.somali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Let us keep in touch.” si ay u muujiso “aan xiriirka sii wadno”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: ending a conversation naturally • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: It was nice talking to you.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: It was nice talking to you..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I am afraid I have to go.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I am afraid I have to go..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I will speak to you soon.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I will speak to you soon..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan ending a conversation naturally. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan ending a conversation naturally. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Dhammeystirka wada sheekeysiga: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka transition + final detail + closing, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Dhibaatooyinka wada xiriirka waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Social Communication Problems.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: A misunderstanding about the meeting time was solved with a short message.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Isfaham la’aan ku saabsan waqtiga kulanka ayaa lagu xalliyay fariin gaaban.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: problem. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa isfaham la’aan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Confusion” while solving a simple communication problem and chose a suitable response.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Confusion”, oo macnihiisu yahay “jahawareer”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: problem. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa jahawareer iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer chose “Incorrect information” while solving a simple communication problem because it matched the situation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Incorrect information” si ay u muujiso “macluumaad khaldan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.

vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Wrong person” while solving a simple communication problem, then showed its natural collocation.

grammar.titleEnglish: Social Communication Problems: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Dhibaatooyinka wada xiriirka
grammar.explanationSomali: Name the problem, apologize if needed, clarify the details, and agree on a practical next step. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: Name the problem, apologize if needed, clarify the details, and agree on a practical next step.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan solving a simple communication problem. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan solving a simple communication problem. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Dhibaatooyinka wada xiriirka: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka problem + clarification/apology + solution, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Qorista codsi edeb leh waxaa lagu sharxayaa Af-Soomaali cad iyadoo la adeegsanayo context, tone iyo jawaab dabiici ah.
learningObjectives: Faham phrases-ka iyo communication function-ka Writing a Polite Request.
Kala saar formal, neutral iyo informal marka ay khuseyso.
Dooro response keliya oo context-ka ugu habboon.
Samee speaking iyo writing practice adigoon bixin xog dhab ah.

vocabulary.somaliMeaning: fadlan ma ii soo diri kartaa...?
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Could you please send me the updated schedule?” while writing a short request for course information, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Could you please send me the updated schedule?” si ay u muujiso “fadlan ma ii soo diri kartaa...?”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: written request. Tone: polite. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa fadlan ma ii soo diri kartaa...? iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Learners practised the term “Updated schedule” while writing a short request for course information and chose a suitable response.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Updated schedule”, oo macnihiisu yahay “jadwalka la cusboonaysiiyay”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: request detail. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa jadwalka la cusboonaysiiyay iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina wrote, “I need to confirm which lessons take place next week.” in her request.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Aamina waxay codsigeeda ku qortay: “Waxaan u baahanahay inaan xaqiijiyo casharrada dhacaya toddobaadka dambe.”
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: context. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waxaan u baahanahay inaan xaqiijiyo... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: In the fictional role-play, Hodan said, “I would appreciate it if you could reply before Thursday.” while writing a short request for course information.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “I would appreciate it if you could reply before Thursday.” si ay u muujiso “waan uga mahadcelin lahaa haddii...”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: appreciation. Tone: formal. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa waan uga mahadcelin lahaa haddii... iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The trainer explained “Before Thursday” while writing a short request for course information, then showed its natural collocation.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Before Thursday”, oo macnihiisu yahay “Khamiista ka hor”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: deadline. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa Khamiista ka hor iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Request information”, oo macnihiisu yahay “macluumaad codso”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: writing purpose. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa macluumaad codso iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: The role-play included “Ask for assistance” while writing a short request for course information, so the meaning was clear.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Ask for assistance”, oo macnihiisu yahay “caawimo codso”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: writing purpose. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa caawimo codso iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, ardaydu waxay barteen “Ask for clarification”, oo macnihiisu yahay “caddayn codso”, kadibna waxay ku adeegsadeen context cad.

grammar.titleEnglish: Writing a Polite Request: language pattern
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka hadalka: Qorista codsi edeb leh
grammar.explanationSomali: A polite written request includes context, one clear request, necessary detail, appreciation, and a suitable closing. Sharaxaaddani waxay ku ekaanaysaa A2, waxayna muujinaysaa communication function-ka, response-ka iyo tone-ka.
grammar.rule: A polite written request includes context, one clear request, necessary detail, appreciation, and a suitable closing.
grammar.sentenceStructure: greeting + context + could request + detail + thanks + closing

dialogues.titleSomali: Wada-hadalka 1: writing a short request for course information • Tone: neutral • Vocabulary iyo comprehension note

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Could you please send me the updated schedule?
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Could you please send me the updated schedule?.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: I need to confirm which lessons take place next week.
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: I need to confirm which lessons take place next week..

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I understand. Could you tell me a little more?
lines.somali: Waan fahmay. Wax yar oo dheeraad ah ma ii sheegi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Before Thursday
lines.somali: Aamina waxay adeegsatay: Before Thursday.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Thank you. That response is clear and appropriate.
lines.somali: Mahadsanid. Jawaabtaasi waa cad oo ku habboon.

lessons.speakingPractice: Samee role-play ku saabsan writing a short request for course information. Adeegso ugu yaraan afar phrase, muuji tone-ka, oo bixi response ku habboon. Xogta oo dhan ha noqoto mala-awaal.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor fariin ama wada-hadal gaaban oo ku saabsan writing a short request for course information. Ku dar opening, communication function, detail iyo closing ku habboon.
lessons.summarySomali: Qorista codsi edeb leh: waxaad baratay phrases-ka, qaabka greeting + context + could request + detail + thanks + closing, tone-ka iyo sida response dabiici ah loogu doorto context cad.
